channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: In Arizona's desert, tiny ants turn into living hygienists, climbing inside bigger ants' mandibles and cleaning them - Phys.org
item.description:
1. In Arizona's desert, tiny ants turn into living hygienists, climbing inside bigger ants' mandibles and cleaning them  Phys.org

  4. Cleaner ants tending to harvester ant (IMAGE)  EurekAlert!
  5. Smithsonian Research Associate Discovers Ants Assemble To Be Picked Clean by ‘Cleaner’ Ants, a Novel Insect Behavior  Smithsonian Institution

1. Astronomers may have finally found where universe’s ‘missing’ hydrogen is hidden  Yahoo News Canada
  2. Astronomers thought the early universe was full of hydrogen: Now they've found it  Phys.org
  3. “There are All Kinds of Neat, Fun, Weird Things Waiting for Us to Find”: Astronomers Discover Giant Hydrogen ‘Halos’ Surrounding Ancient Galaxies  The Debrief
  4. Tracking the Enormous Hydrogen Gas Halos of Ancient Galaxies  AZoQuantum
  5. After Decades of Searching, Astronomers Finally Track Down the Universe’s Missing Hydrogen  SciTechDaily

item.title: Californian Hybrid Honeybee Population Has Evolved Natural Defense against Varroa Mites: Study - Sci.News
item.description:
1. Californian Hybrid Honeybee Population Has Evolved Natural Defense against Varroa Mites: Study  Sci.News
  2. SoCal's hybrid bees outsmart Varroa mites before they even hatch  Phys.org
  3. Unique Honeybees Found Only in Southern California Can Fend Off Deadly Varroa Mites  Good News Network
  4. Researchers in beekeeping suits (IMAGE)  EurekAlert!
  5. Southern California bees are keeping deadly mites in check  The Brighter Side of News

item.title: Africa’s forests have flipped from carbon sink to carbon source - ScienceDaily
item.description: Africa’s forests have flipped from carbon sink to carbon source  ScienceDaily
